title: UBSan: Misaligned pointer upcast in ART index when creating UNIQUE INDEX with repeated columns
description:
### What happens?

Creating a UNIQUE INDEX with a large number of repeated columns causes Undefined Behavior in DuckDB’s ART index implementation.
When running with UBSan enabled, DuckDB reports a misaligned pointer upcast in prefix.cpp, which may lead to crashes or incorrect behavior.

### To Reproduce

```
pragma enable_verification;
 CREATE TABLE v0 ( v2 TEXT , v1 INT ) ; INSERT INTO v0 ( v2 , v1 ) VALUES ( '358677 4 2 1' , 7 ) ,( 'a%' , 1 ) ; CREATE UNIQUE INDEX v3 ON v0 ( v1 , v1 , v1 , v1 , v1 , v2 , v1 , v2 , v1 , v2 , v2 , v1 , v2 , v2 , v2 , v2 , v2 , v2 , v1 , v1 , v2 , v2 , v1 , v1 , v2 , v1 ) ;
```

### OS:

Ubuntu 22.04.1 LTS x86_64

### DuckDB Version:

DuckDB v1.4.1-dev211 (Development Version) 4f797f8

### DuckDB Client:

duckdb executable

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

smartfuzz

### Affiliation:

smartfuzz

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE v1 ( id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, value REAL );
INSERT INTO v1 ( id, name, value ) VALUES ( 1, 'test1', 1.5 ), ( 2, 'test2', NULL );
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_value ON v1 ( value, name, id );
SELECT * FROM v1 WHERE value IS NULL OR name LIKE 'test%';
CREATE TABLE v2 ( col1 INTEGER, col2 TEXT, col3 DATE DEFAULT (date('now')) );
key_bug_features: Creating UNIQUE INDEX with repeated columns causes misaligned pointer upcast
